entry.title: Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
entry.content:
Why do we need more AI in SAP Build?

For a very simple reason: our users want to be even more efficient in delivering innovations and SAP extensions. Our customers want to accelerate application development and process automation with the help of AI to get more time to focus on what really matters instead of spilling time on tedious and repetitive tasks.

Let me outline some of the problems our customers describe:

  • “As a business process expert, I want to get assistance to automate my processes and critical workflow much faster”.
  • “I’m an application developer, and I'd like to stop spending time on tedious tasks like creating data models and sample data to test my apps. And if AI could help me create some basic JavaScript code, not rocket science, that would be great!”
  • “For a software developer like me, it would be great if we could efficiently code applications and concentrate on the value-add coding part, so I can deliver on my projects faster”

At SAP, we’re committed to delivering AI capabilities that make it even better to develop with SAP Build. Just a month ago, we announced the general availability of our Joule copilot for ABAP developers: now Joule speaks ABAP!  Today we proudly announced new Joule capabilities for developers. In the next few paragraphs, I will take you on a deep dive into the new capabilities you can leverage to benefit from the power of (generative) Artificial Intelligence for building applications or automating business processes.

First, we identified a few key themes where AI has a significant impact in the realm of app development and process automation:

  • Application Generation: Generate CAP & RAP applications from scratch including data models, sample data, unit tests and more
  • Code Optimization: Generate and optimize JavaScript, Fiori/UI5 code with predictive code completion, code search, and code explanation
  • Artifacts Generation: Generate and summarize complex artifacts such as processes, data types, scripts, decisions, as well as approval recommendations

All these capabilities significantly accelerate application development and process automation and – let’s be honest – make it more fun, also.

Now let’s explore together how AI will assist you across all SAP Build.

SAP Build Process Automation

The purpose of AI and generative AI capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation is very simple yet powerful: assisting our customers in the implementation of their process automations. For that, the design capabilities leverage generative AI to allow users to interactively generate and edit artifacts from natural language description, as well as generating summaries of existing artifacts.

With that, we create immediate value for our customers:

  • Building artefacts such as processes, forms and business rules, and automating processes with the help of AI based on natural language input help increase developer productivity, improve quality and enable further innovations.

  • Summarizing processes, workflows and complex artefacts such as Business Decisions (Rules) using natural business language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for documenting processes.

We also added two additional AI-based capabilities that make process automation better:

  • DOX Premium service is integrated with SAP Build bringing a paradigm shift in document processing automation world. Generative Al-based document understanding not only integrates cutting-edge natural language processing capabilities but also holds the promise of eliminating the necessity for the laborious and costly endeavor of template creation and maintenance.
  • SAP Task Center's intelligent approval recommendation capability enables faster and better decisions based on machine learning, streamlining operations, and improving efficiency with AI-driven insights based on patterns observed in other approval tasks.

To get a concrete view on how AI works in SAP Build Process Automation, watch our 7 minutes video demo on Intelligent Processing of Sales Orders with Enterprise Automation.

SAP Build Apps

There is also a new AI-based capability in our no-code development environment. In SAP Build Apps we now have UI generation from data. The UI of an application is often directly related to the structure of the data handled by the application, so AI can propose Us based on data. In the screenshot below you can see data entities that are part of the application – in this case, Books, Authors and Genres – and right next to these, the UI pages were created by AI.

In order to activate this Generative AI capability in SAP Build Apps, you have to 

  1. Click on the "Generative AI" tile in your SAP Build tenant configuration:

  2. Enable Generative AI feature with the toggle:



SAP Build Code

Many AI capabilities have been introduced during the last months in SAP Build Code. Let me share a recap with you:

Joule supports complex multi-steps tasks for advanced CAP application generation

As of early 2025, the optimal approach for professional developers working with Generative AI involves creating project skeletons that cover general requirements, followed by incremental enhancements. SAP Build Code Joule expands on this practice with multi-tasks support, allowing developers to use a single prompt to engage specialized agents for various tasks. By coordinating these agents, Joule creates more comprehensive project foundations, thereby streamlining the development process. This approach enables developers to focus more on refining and customizing their applications, improving productivity and efficiency.

As an example, when a developer asks Joule for assistance in creating an application, an execution plan is displayed. The complex requirements are broken down into subtasks to generate

  1. the data model
  2. the sample data
  3. the application logic

and we apply our best practices for each step. The developer can review the improved user requirements and enhance them manually, via the pencil button.

For more details, read our blog post SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation.

Joule for mobile app development

Joule now also supports mobile app development in SAP Build Code: it uses mobile services, like MDK action generation.

Find more in this blog post from Jitendra Kansal: “Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule” and for additional details, read this technical documentation. 

Joule for searching and analyzing existing code

With Joule copilot, you can now use natural language queries to quickly and easily locate specific code snippets. You can simply ask Joule what you're looking for, and it will instantly show you the files relevant to your search criteria, saving you time and effort in your coding journey.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition.

Joule for SAP HANA Machine Learning

The SQL console provided by the SAP HANA Database Explorer extension in SAP Build Code supports SQL statement generation via natural language. Users can enter a text prompt and use the new run method “Run Prompt” to generate a SQL statement that can be executed on the SAP HANA Cloud database. The generated statement can be executed like any other statement directly from the SQL console.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition

Joule for Fiori tools and UI5 guided development

SAP Build Code also offers a variety of generative AI functionalities to support UI5 development. Joule can now be used to both accelerate the development and provide support for those who may not be UI5 experts.

For more details, read our blog posts UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule and SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule. 

The business user view: SAP Build Work Zone and AI

Of course, beyond the developer, we also investigate how we can make the SAP Build business user more efficient with the help of AI. So, while the below update is not part of our Joule for Developers capabilities, I still wanted to mention this and provide our readers the chance to dive deeper.

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities

This enables users to interact with Joule and get answers from all connected systems​. An example would be that you're trying to track the status of a purchase order. Instead of navigating through multiple screens, you simply ask Joule, “Has my purchase order been approved?”. Joule instantly provides the status and key details directly within its window in SAP Build Work Zone, this seamless interaction eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and effort.

For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, read @Yumnahabbasi's blog post: “Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!”

Feedback from Early Adopters: Up to 40% cost reduction!

Now, back to the developer view. We gathered feedback from beta users to understand the impact these capabilities have on their application development and process automation.  The results they achieved in terms of productivity speak for themselves:

  • Up to 40%​ cost reduction for ERP extension migration: it enables customers to review and transition existing extensions faster and more efficiently into the cloud. It simplifies future upgrades by providing side-by-side extensions that keep the ERP core clean
  • Up to 30% reduction in low-code process automation effort: building and automating processes based on natural language input, allows for increased developer productivity, improved quality and more innovations.
  • Up to 30%​ cost reduction for pro-code application development: generating code based on natural-language input and pre-trained code, and data generation model, allows for faster development, quicker migration to newer digital environments and improved development capacity for innovations.
  • Up to 10% time savings for process developers: summarizing processes and complex artefacts using natural language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for understanding processes.
  • Up to 10% faster time to market for new low-code applications: empowering developers to build applications and automate processes faster based on natural language input accelerates projects.

Join our Webcast

We've set up a webcast where I'll have the pleasure to be showing you AI in SAP Build in action, plus you'll have the chance to bring me and other product managers any questions you might have.

Join us on April 10, 2025!

  • APJ: 2:00 PM SGT (Singapore) / 4:00 PM AEST (Sydney)
  • EMEA: 2:00 PM CET (Central Europe) / 1:00 PM GMT (UK)
  • NA: 2:00 PM EST / 11:00 AM PST (Pacific)

You can also

  • Watch our Joule for Developers video
  • Attend our LinkedIn Live event on March 25th at 9AM PST
  • Participate in our SAP Community contest 
  • Check out the official FAQs on Joule for developers
  • Attend our SAP Community Ask Me Anything session in April (link to come soon)

Enjoy AI and Joule in SAP Build!

entry.title: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • An overview of guided experiences
  • Updated Features and Content
    • Restarting Guided Experiences
    • Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences
    • Collapsible Sections
    • Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Conclusion
  • Additional Read

An overview of guided experiences

In our last blog, we elaborated how Guided Experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Powered by SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation, Guided Experiences can help navigate through complex business processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hire or supplier onboarding, training and enablement, or browsing and & purchasing electronic equipment. Users can model intuitive stages and stages which can by further enriched with content, documents, forms, UI cards and more. Please see our previous blog to learn more about modelling a Guided Experience directly in SAP Build Process Automation from a Guided Process Artefact, creating stages and steps within each stage , deploying to Work Zone and connecting Guided Process as Wizard template in SAP Build Work Zone. In this blog, we will elaborate on the recently released advanced capabilities like multi-instance support, restarting a guided experience, having collapsible sections and pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors.

Updated Features and Content

Restarting Guided Experiences

One of the key updates is the ability to restart guided experiences. This feature allows users to reset and begin the experience from the start, providing flexibility and ensuring that users can correct any mistakes made during the initial run. The restart feature is also very helpful for easily demoing guided experiences. It allows presenters to quickly reset the process and demonstrate the guided experience multiple times without having to manually undo each step.
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Restart Button
The restart functionality is implemented as a button within the guided experience cover. When users click this button, they are prompted with a warning message that informs them that all progress will be lost, and the guided experience will start over from the beginning. 

Restart Guided Experience

A warning message is provided as a confirmation step, preventing accidental restarts / data loss and ensuring that users make an informed decision:
Warning message when restarting guided experience

Enable/Disable Setting
To provide further customization, the restart feature can be enabled or disabled per wizard. This setting allows administrators to control whether the restart option is available for specific guided experiences. Depending on the use case, restarting may not be applicable or feasible, in which cases this option can be disabled in the wizard settings.
Setting to enable restart

Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences

Another significant enhancement is the introduction of multi-instance support. This feature enables multiple instances of a guided experience to run simultaneously, allowing users to manage and track various processes concurrently. This is very useful for scenarios where multiple tasks need to be handled in parallel.Multi-instance overview for guided experience
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Use Cases
Multi-instance support can be applied to various guided experience use cases, including but not limited to:

  • Event Planning - Organizing multiple events simultaneously, each with its own set of tasks and requirements.
  • Purchasing IT Equipment - Managing multiple purchase requests for IT equipment, ensuring that each request is tracked and processed independently.
  • Grievances Requests - Handling multiple grievance requests concurrently, allowing for efficient resolution and tracking of each request.

Managing and Viewing All Instances
To facilitate the management of multiple instances, users can view all active instances in a dedicated interface. This interface provides options to:

  • Add Name - Assign a unique name to each instance for easy identification.
  • Restart Instance - Reset an instance to its initial state, allowing users to start the process over.
  • Cancel Instance - Terminate a given guided experience instance, removing it from the active list.

Multi-instance detail view for guided experience

Enable / Disable Setting
Similar to the restart feature, multi-instance support can be enabled or disabled per guided experience. This setting allows workpage owners to control whether the multi-instance option is available for the specific guided experience. Depending on the use case, running multiple instances may not be applicable or feasible, and this option can be disabled in the wizard settings in such a scenario.

Setting to enable multi-instance

Collapsible Sections

The collapsible sections feature is designed to improve the user interface for guided experiences and freestyle sections alike, providing flexibility for both enduser & content creators working with workpages.


Section Settings
Each workpage section comes with dedicated settings, including the option to make it collapsible. This allows users to personalize different sections based on their preferences and needs. Sections can be set to either expanded or collapsed by default, providing a tailored experience for each scenario. A typical use case is creating an FAQ page with many sections where the collapsed by default structure the workpage content nicely.


Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Freestyle Layout
For freestyle layout sections, it is recommended to include a title or information for the dropdown. The title and information act as a header, making it clear what each section contains when collapsed.

Wizard Layout
In guided experiences, collapsible sections support various scenarios such as starting an instance, continuing an instance, and more. These collapsed sections also include a progress bar to visually indicate the user's progress of the guided experience. This feature is particularly useful for placing guided experiences space-efficiently on workpages without cluttering the interface. Example guided experiences in collapsed view:

Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors

We are excited to introduce pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors, designed to streamline HR processes and enhance user experience. This content includes ready-to-use templates for employee onboarding and cross-boarding content packages, which can be easily deployed in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation.

Content Availability
The pre-built content for Process Automation is available in the SAP Build Lobby store, while the content for Work Zone is provided as dedicated content packages via the Work Zone admin UI. These packages - among many other items - also feature a re-usable, configurable UI Card for item lists, allowing for a more personalized user experience for both users and content creators alike.

Scenarios of Content
There are three scenarios of pre-built content available:

  • Legacy (UI5 component card + workflow) - This scenario includes a single UI5 component card per guided experience combined with a workflow. It is however not recommended for new setups due to its outdated structure, limited flexibility & complex modification approach.
  • Work Zone Wizard - This scenario involves a Work Zone local wizard template, including UI cards. It provides a streamlined and intuitive experience for users, allowing them to navigate through processes with ease.
  • Process Automation version - This scenario is the combined version of Work Zone and Process Automation content. It leverages the strengths of both platforms to provide a comprehensive solution for managing complex processes.

For more detailed instructions on how to leverage this pre-built content, please refer to the following SAP Help guide. In this guide we also provide a detailed comparison table of those three scenarios of guided experience pre-built content.

 

Conclusion

The guided experiences are changing the way businesses can manage complex processes. From creating end-to-end onboarding experience for new employees to the application of parental leave, guided experiences are leveraging various capabilities within SAP Build as a deeply integrated, unified solution experience.

 

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone: How to Create a Wizard
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard
  • Video example Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences
  • 2024 Devtoberfest Guided Experience recording: https://www.youtube.com/live/q1pmO283Y5c?si=wfNm8eDNJPC0t_k8

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Build continues to evolve to support how modern teams work — now with more artificial intelligence, flexibility, and speed. In Q1 2025, we delivered a wave of innovations across SAP Build Code, Process Automation, Apps, and Work Zone to help developers and business users turn ideas into enterprise-ready solutions faster than ever.

Whether you're building custom apps, creating SAP S/4HANA extensions, or streamlining processes, these updates will help you move from concept to value more efficiently.  Let's review the top 10 updates we selected from last quarter.

Licensing and Availability Updates

1. SAP Task Center Now included with SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Task Center is now embedded in all SAP Build Work Zone editions, giving users a unified view of workflow tasks from multiple SAP systems — right within their Work Zone homepage. No separate integration is required. This update also extends to partner licensing, making including Task Center in custom Work Zone scenarios easier. The result is faster approvals, centralized task tracking, and higher productivity for business users and managers — all in one workspace.

R

2. SAP Build Apps Now Available in More Regions

You can now deploy SAP Build Apps in EU11 and Sydney data centers, bringing your applications to more users. The increased coverage improves app responsiveness, supports region-specific compliance needs, and enables global scalability for your enterprise use cases.

Joule for Developers Updates

3. Generate End-to-End Workflows with a Prompt

In SAP Build Process Automation, developers can now describe business scenarios in natural language — and let generative AI create forms, decisions, and process flows automatically. Powered by SAP's purpose-built large language models (LLMs), this capability delivers contextualized, SAP-aware outcomes that speed up workflow creation and reduce manual effort. It also empowers business users to contribute to process design without writing code.

4. Generate UI Pages from Data in SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps now uses generative AI to propose user interface pages based on your data model. For example, if your app contains entities like Books, Authors, or Genres, Joule recommends corresponding screens, layouts, and controls. This helps teams move quickly from data modeling to interactive UI design, shortening prototyping cycles and improving design consistency.

5. Use Generative AI for Fiori UI Creation in SAP Build Code

Developers can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to generate Fiori-based UIs for existing CAP apps by entering the command /add-ui or selecting the Joule icon in the Storyboard. Joule understands your application structure and proposes relevant layouts, controls, and navigation patterns, helping you scaffold front-end apps quickly and focus on customization instead of starting from scratch.

6. Multi-Agent Support for CAP App Generation

SAP Build Code now uses multi-agent support powered by Joule to generate complete CAP applications. Each agent focuses on a specific part — like data models, services, or UI — so you get a more complete project from a single prompt. This enhancement saves time and helps you focus on business logic instead of building everything from scratch.

Read also Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code by david_bizer.

Enhance SAP Business Suite Applications

7. Extend SAP S/4HANA with Custom UIs (Planned for Release Soon)

Coming very soon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, developers will be able to use the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard to create side-by-side extensions—either by customizing existing SAP Fiori apps or building entirely new web applications. This approach enables teams to meet specific business needs while preserving the core system, supporting clean core principles and long-term agility.

  • See documentation: Using the Extensibility Wizard.
  • On setup, read the blog SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by @Karthik-Thiru. 

8. New Enhancements for Guided Experiences by SAP Build

New enhancements to Guided Experiences by SAP Build help simplify complex processes like employee onboarding, parental leave, procurement approvals, and IT service management, where multiple steps, systems, and stakeholders are involved. With this release, ready-to-use content packages for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding and Cross-boarding are now available in the SAP Build Store—enabling faster rollout of consistent, step-by-step guidance for new hires and internal transfers.

  • Read more: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level by florian_buech.
  • Watch the 2-minute video: Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences.

9. Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger

With new support for intermediate message events, SAP Build Process Automation lets workflows pause until specific API-triggered inputs are received and resume automatically. This powerful capability reduces wait times and manual monitoring, helping teams design more adaptive and responsive processes with fewer errors and overhead. 

See documentation: Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger | SAP Help Portal

Boost Developer Productivity

10. Revamped Project Overview in SAP Build Code

A redesigned Project Overview in SAP Build Code provides a clean, tree-based view of all app components, supporting CAP Java and CAP Node. This enhanced layout integrates with the Storyboard, making it easier for developers to navigate complex projects, onboard new team members, and stay focused while building.

See documentation: Project Overview | SAP Help Portal

Ready to See These Updates in Action?

These are just a few of the top updates rolling out this quarter. Want to explore the new features in more detail, see live demos, and get your questions answered by product experts?

👉Join us on April 15 for the What's New in SAP Build Q1 2025 webinar.
Reserve your spot to discover these updates in action. Register below:

📅There are two options to cover different time zones.
🕘9:00 – 10:00 AM CET → https://sap.to/604200bfg
🕔5:00 – 6:00 PM CET → https://sap.to/604500bfc

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • SAP Build Help Portal
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

Please stay current and connect with fellow SAP Builders by joining our Application Development and Automation Community for the latest updates, activities, and challenges.

entry.title: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal have been deprecated for many years. Now the time has come to optimize our web UI technology by removing these themes. We plan to remove them in the next releases of products which allow users to choose multiple themes, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Over the years SAP Fiori has evolved, introducing new visual themes along the way – most recently the Horizon theme. The Horizon theme not only looks modern and appealing, it also provides the highest level of support for accessibility, as well as the most flexibility for customers to create their own custom themes.

Horizon is the visual theme used by all our products in the SAP Business Suite. Figures 1 – 3 below show some examples of Horizon in our products.

We strongly recommend that all users use the Horizon theme to ensure an optimal and consistent user experience.

 

Removal of Belize and Blue Crystal

The visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal will be removed from SAP products with their next releases, starting in the second half of 2025. This means that the upcoming releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning, as well as further products which currently include one or both of these themes, will no longer provide them as options when running in web browsers.

After upgrading, existing usages of Belize or Blue Crystal will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.

If you have created your own custom theme based on Belize or Blue Crystal using the UI theme designer, you will need to migrate this to an available theme. Our strong recommendation is to base the new version on the Horizon theme.

For more information about the web UI technologies affected, and how to migrate custom themes, have a look at this blog post by Oliver Graeff:

  • Announcement: Removal of Belize theme of SAP Fiori.

Additional Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Important: If you use a custom theme and have set up the UI Theme Designer connection to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a communication arrangement on SAP Business Technology Platform Neo, make sure to move it to Cloud Foundry before you upgrade. Otherwise the automatic redirect to Horizon will not work. Also, future innovations will require the communication arrangement to use Cloud Foundry, so doing this move makes sense anyway. Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Integrating UI Theme Designer – SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

 

Examples of SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

You can see some examples for Horizon in figures 1 – 3: for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (the images show SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502) and SAP SuccessFactors. For further examples, have a look at these blog posts:

  • SAP Fiori Update: More Products with Horizon, Entry Point strategy, UX news for SAP S/4HANA and More.
  • SAP User Experience Update: Yet More Products with the New Look driven by SAP Fiori.
  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori.
  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.
  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More) – along with parts 2 – 7 which are linked therein.

Figure 1: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start. ALT Text: the image shows a screenshot of SAP Start with the Horizon theme, three iPhones with Horizon (one dark and one light showing SAP Mobile start, one showing an iPhone start screen with a notification from SAP Mobile STart ), and an Apple Watch with a notification from SAP Mobile Start and Horizon.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ALT Text: an image showing two sceens with Morning Horizon (the My Home page and Monitor Purchase Order Items analytical list page) and two screens with the same apps with Evening Horizon, plus the text “Also available for accessibility: high contrast light and dark themes”.

Figure 3: Examples of SAP SuccessFactors applications with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: Three examples showing both web and mobile: on the left the Home Page, in the middle Team Goals, on the right Opportunity Marketplace showing many cards, each displaying an opportunity.

To find out more about the Horizon theme, have a look at the SAP Fiori for web design guidelines:

  • Theming.

entry.title: AI活用でも必須のSAP環境におけるグローバルユーザーIDとSAP IAS設定方針ガイド
entry.content:
はじめに

現代の企業において、業務のクラウド化・多様化、そしてAIによる高度化が進む中、複数のSAP製品を横断して利用するケースが増えています。こうした環境では、ユーザーごとの一貫したID管理が求められ、複数のシステム間で同一人物を正確に識別し、適切な認証・権限付与を行うことが重要になります。まさにSAPのAIデジタルアシスタントのJouleやSAP Task Centerも対象の一つです。

本ガイドでは、SAPのクラウド基盤であるSAP Cloud Identity Servicesの重要な一機能である「グローバルユーザーID」の役割と仕組みに加え、その実装を支えるSAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication（SAP IAS）の設定方法について解説します。特に、SAP SuccessFactorsやSAP S/4HANAといった主要なSAP製品との連携において、ユーザー情報の統合とシングルサインオンの実現をどのように行うかに焦点を当て、実践的なステップや注意点を紹介します。

 

1. グローバルユーザーIDとは何か

グローバルユーザーIDとは、SAPが提供する各種ビジネスアプリケーションやサービスを含む広範なランドスケープの中で、特定のユーザーを一意に識別するための共通IDのことを意味します​。*1

このグローバルユーザーIDを持たないシステム構成では、システムAからシステムBにアクセスする際に、それぞれのシステムでのどのユーザーがどのユーザーと同じ人物であるのかを認識するために、それ相応の処理を実装する必要がありました。

 

しかしグローバルユーザーIDという概念と実装を導入することにより、こうした煩雑なIDマッピングに必要性を解消できます​。技術的にはUUID（Universally Unique Identifier）/ GUID (Global Unique Identifier)形式で生成される識別子であり、例えば、SAP Cloud Identity Services 上のユーザーストアである Identity Directory service（以下IdDSとして言及します) 上でユーザーが作成される際に自動的に割り当てられます​。

このグローバルユーザーIDは「関連付け属性 (correlation attribute)」として機能し、複数のシステムに存在するユーザーアカウント同士を裏側で紐付ける役割を果たします​。

例えば、ある従業員がSAP SuccessFactorsとSAP S/4HANAの両方にユーザーアカウントを持つ場合、グローバルユーザーIDによってその人物であることが一意に結び付けられます。これにより、直接的にやり取りを行うシステムにとどまらず、その他のシステムも、特定の処理が同じユーザーに関連して行われていることを相互に認識でき、ユーザー情報やタスクの統合参照が可能となります​。グローバルユーザーIDはあくまで内部的なIDであり、エンドユーザーがログイン時に入力するユーザー名やメールアドレスとは別物ですが、システム連携の中核となる重要な要素です。

 

2. なぜ今、グローバルユーザーIDが必要なのか？

現代のSAPを取り巻く環境では、複数のクラウドサービスやオンプレミスシステムを統合し、ユーザーにシームレスな体験を提供することが求められています。しかし各システムでユーザーIDがばらばらな状態では、ユーザーごとにデータやタスクを統合する際に対応関係を取ることが非常に困難となります。そこで、グローバルユーザーIDという単一の識別子を導入して効果的に利用することで、システム境界を越えたユーザー関連データの統合という課題に対処し、企業全体で一貫したユーザー識別を実現することができるのです。結果として、一度の認証で複数のSAPサービスにアクセスできるシングルサインオン (SSO) や、一貫した権限管理が可能となります。

具体的なユースケースとして、SAP Task Centerがあります。*2 SAP Task Centerは様々なSAPアプリケーション（例: SAP SuccessFactorsなど）からユーザー宛てのワークフロー承認依頼やタスクを集約し、一つの受信トレイに統合表示するサービスです​ 。ここで鍵となるのがユーザーの一貫性であり、異なるバックエンドシステムから取得したタスクが同一人物に紐付いていることを保証する必要があります。グローバルユーザーIDがその共通鍵として機能し、各システムから集めたタスクを正しいユーザーに関連付ける役割を担います​ 。もしグローバルユーザーIDによる統一がなければ、システム間でユーザーIDを突き合わせる複雑なロジックが必要となり、タスクの漏れや誤割り当てが発生しかねません。

 

 

また、SAPが提供する新しいAIデジタルアシスタントであるJouleの利用シナリオでもグローバルユーザーIDが重要です。JouleはSAP Business Technology Platform上で動作し、複数のバックエンドシステム（例えばSAP S/4HANA CloudやSAP SuccessFactorsなど）と連携してユーザーの作業支援を行います​。*4

 

その前提として、各システムでユーザーが同一人物であることを認識できなければなりません。実際、Jouleを有効化するためには連携する全ての製品上で同じユーザーのグローバルユーザーIDが存在していることが必要条件となっています​。*5 このように、SAP Task CenterやJouleのような横断的サービスでは統合認証基盤としてグローバルユーザーIDが不可欠であり、各ソリューション間でスムーズにユーザーコンテキストを共有する鍵となっています。

 

3. セットアップの概要

グローバルユーザーIDの導入とSAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (通称 SAP IAS、以下SAP IASとして言及) との連携設定を行う際の概要ステップを以下に整理します。

  1. 前提準備: まず、グローバルユーザーIDを活用するためにシステム環境を整備します。SAP Cloud Identity Servicesのテナントが用意されていること、各SAP製品（例: SAP SuccessFactorsやSAP S/4HANAなど）がSAP IAS連携に対応したバージョン・設定になっていることを確認します。

  2. ユーザーIDの一元化とプロビジョニング: 次に、ユーザーアカウント情報の一元管理を設計していきます。各システムに分散して存在するユーザーを統合するため、SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning (通称SAP IPS、以下SAP IPSとして言及) などのツールを用いてユーザー情報を同期させます。例えば、人事システムであるSAP SuccessFactorsをユーザーのマスタとして扱う場合には、SAP IPS経由でSAP Cloud Identity Services のユーザーストアである IdDS にユーザーをプロビジョニングすることで、同時にグローバルユーザーIDを生成・付与します​。SAP IPS のSCIMプロビジョニングでは、SAP拡張スキーマの一部であるurn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User 内のuserUuid属性（グローバルユーザーID）が各ユーザーに含まれるため、これを用いて全システムで共通のIDを保持できます​。*1
    このように、マスタソースから IdDS へユーザーを流す形でプロビジョニングを行うと、各ユーザーに統一されたグローバルユーザーIDが割り当てられ、他のシステムへも同じIDを展開できます。その他のSAP IPSの利用シナリオでは、SAP SuccessFactorsやSAP S/4HANA CloudからSAP Build Work Zone（SAP BTP上の統合ポータルサービス）に対し、ユーザーのメールアドレス・グローバルユーザーID・所属グループなどを同期する、といったパターンが取られることもあります。

  3. SAP IASでの認証設定: SAP IASを各システムの認証基盤（IdP）とするための信頼関係とユーザー属性マッピングを構成します。具体的には、SAP IAS管理コンソールで各アプリケーション（接続先システム）についてのSAML 2.0設定を開き、ユーザー識別に使用する属性を設定します。グローバルユーザーIDによる認証連携を行う場合、SAP IAS側でSAMLアサーション属性の一つとしてuser_uuid（表示名「Global User Id」）が含まれていることを確認する必要があります。もし既定で存在しない場合は、「カスタム属性の追加」機能からIdentity Directoryをソースとしてuser_uuid属性を追加し、その値にグローバルユーザーIDをマッピングする必要があります。*6 この設定により、ユーザーがSAP IASで認証された際にグローバルユーザーIDがSAMLトークンに含まれ、連携先のアプリケーション側でユーザーを一意に特定できるようになります​。なお、EmailやLogin Nameといった他の属性も必要に応じてアサーションに含めますが、システム横断のユーザーの突合処理にはグローバルユーザーID（user_uuid）がキー項目となります。

  4. 各システムでの受入設定: 最後に、個々のSAP製品側でSAP IASから受け取ったユーザーを正しくマッピングする設定を行います。SAP SuccessFactorsの場合、SAP IASと統合認証を行うことでSAP SuccessFactors内のユーザーにグローバルユーザーIDが関連付けられます（SAP IAS上で生成されたIDがSAP SuccessFactorsユーザーに対応付けられる）ため、特別な追加設定は不要です。SAP S/4HANA等のシステムでも、クラウド製品であればSAP IASとのトラスト設定を行い、Name IDや属性としてグローバルユーザーIDを用いるよう設定します。オンプレミスのSAP S/4HANAをSAP Task Centerに接続するようなケースでは、ABAPユーザー管理(例えばSU01)上でグローバルユーザーID（SAPUSER_UUID）が取得できることを確認し、それを用いてTask Center側でユーザーを紐付けます。各システム固有の設定手順については製品ごとのガイドに委ねられますが、要点は「SAP IASから渡されたグローバルユーザーIDをキーとしてユーザーを識別する」ように受入れ側を調整することです。

 

4. セットアップにおける課題・考慮点

グローバルユーザーIDとSAP IASを設定する際によく直面する課題や注意すべきポイントを以下にまとめます。

  • ID概念の混同に注意すること
    SAPシステムには、ユーザーごとに様々なIDや番号（例: ユーザーID、従業員ID、Person ID、User GUIDなど）が存在します。プロジェクトメンバーにはグローバルユーザーIDの目的と他のIDとの違いを周知し、名称の類似による混乱を避ける必要があります。

  • ユーザー同期と一意性の確保
    各システムで同じユーザーに別々のグローバルユーザーIDが発行されてしまう事態は避けねばなりません。グローバルユーザーIDはSAP IASでユーザー作成時に自動生成されますが、例えば同一人物を異なるシステム上で個別に新規登録してしまうと、それぞれに別個のグローバルIDが割り当てられてしまいます。これを防ぐには、前述のように一元的なプロビジョニングでユーザーを管理するか、既存ユーザーの統合時にはメールアドレスや社員IDなど共通のキーで突合して単一のユーザーに紐付ける作業が必要です。特に、最初にSAP IAS側でユーザーを作成してグローバルユーザーIDを発行し、それをもとに他システムへユーザーを連携させるフローにすると安全です。

  • 属性マッピングの誤り
    SAP IASと各アプリケーションの接続設定において、グローバルユーザーIDが正しくマッピングされていないとユーザー対応付けの不整合が発生します。例えば、SAP IAS側でuser_uuidをアサーションに含め忘れたり、受入れ側で別の属性（メールアドレス等）をユーザーキーにしている場合、システム間でユーザーを同一人物とみなせず統合が破綻します。グローバルユーザーIDをユーザー識別の軸として用いる設定になっているかを念入りに確認する必要があります。

  • 運用時のユーザー管理
    グローバルユーザーID導入後は、ユーザーライフサイクル管理にも留意が必要です。ユーザーの入社・退職時には、SAP Cloud Identity Services (の中でもユーザーマスタであるIdDS) 上のユーザーアカウント追加・削除プロセスと、各業務システム側のユーザー管理がきちんと同期しているか確認します。例えば退職者のアカウントを、あるシステムで削除したがSAP IAS上には残っているといった場合、グローバルユーザーIDを介して不要なアクセスが残存するリスクがあります。また、企業内のIDプロバイダ（Entra ID / Microsoft Active Directoryなど）とSAP IASをフェデレーションしている場合、SAP IASをプロキシモードで利用するだけでは自動的にグローバルユーザーIDが各システムに行き渡らないため、別途SAP IPSでの定期同期や初回ログイン時の自動プロビジョニング設定を検討してください。

 

最後に

以上、SAP環境におけるグローバルユーザーIDとSAP IAS設定の概要とポイントを解説しました。複数のSAPソリューションを統合的に利用する際には、グローバルユーザーIDによる共通のユーザー基盤が成功の鍵となります。公式のSAP Help Portal ​や、SAP Communityのガイド記事​も参照しつつ、自社のランドスケープに合わせた設計・設定を進めてください。

 

参考文献

  • *1) SAP Help Portal：SAP Cloud Identity Servicesを利用したユーザーIDの連携シナリオおよびその設定ガイド
    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/global-user-id-in-integration-scenarios
  • *2) SAP Help Portal：SAP Task Centerとは？
    https://help.sap.com/docs/task-center/sap-task-center/what-is-sap-task-center?locale=en-US
  • *3) SAP Community Blog：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionのJouleセットアップ手順に関するステップバイステップガイド
    https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453
  • *4) SAP Help Portal：Jouleとは？
    https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/3fdd7b321eb24d1b9d40605dce822e84/38636457acc346daa4ff7069f041a11a.html
  • *5) SAP Help Portal：JouleをSAP製品と連携する際の前提条件
    https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=services%20tenant%20and%20within%20all,based%20application%20and%20relies%20on
  • *6) SAP Help Portal：Jouleの設定手順 SAP Cloud Identity Servicesとの連携設定
    https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=groups%20Groups%20user_uuid%20Global%20User,Save%20your%20configuration

entry.title: Global User ID and SAP IAS: Essentials for AI integration in SAP landscape
entry.content:
Introduction

Modern companies are increasingly using SAP across multiple products as their operations are becoming more cloud-based, diversified and advanced with AI. In these environments, consistent identity management for each user is required, and it is important to accurately identify the same person across multiple systems and to provide appropriate authentication and authorization. SAP’s AI Digital Assistant, Joule, and SAP Task Center also require consistent identity management.

This guide describes the role and mechanism of “Global User ID”, an important feature of SAP’s cloud foundation, SAP Cloud Identity Services, as well as how to configure SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (SAP IAS) to support its implementation. In particular, it focuses on how to integrate user information and enable single sign-on in collaboration with key SAP products, such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA, and introduces practical steps and notes.

 

1. What are Global User IDs?

A global user ID is a common ID that uniquely identifies a particular user in a broad landscape, including various business applications and services from SAP. *1

In this system configuration without a global user ID, when accessing System B from System A, it was necessary to implement appropriate actions to know which user in each system was the same person as which user.

 

However, by introducing the concept and implementation of global user ID, you can avoid the complexity of ID mapping across systems. Technically, global user ID is a generated identifier in UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) or GUID (Global Unique Identifier) format, which is automatically assigned when a user is created in the Identity Directory (referred to below as IdDS), the user persistency layer of SAP Cloud Identity Services.

This global user ID acts as a “correlation attribute” and acts as a back-to-back link between user accounts that exist in multiple systems.

For example, if an employee has a user account in both SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA, the global user ID uniquely connects the person. This allows other systems to know that certain actions are being done in relation to the same user, as well as to provide a consolidated reference to user information and tasks. Global user IDs are only internal, separate from the username and email address that end users enter when they log in, but they are a key element of system integration.

 

2. Why do I need a global user ID now?

The modern environment around SAP requires the integration of multiple cloud services and on-premise systems to provide a seamless experience for users. However, with disparate user IDs in each system, it is very difficult to have a corresponding relationship when integrating data and tasks for each user. So, by implementing a single identifier, the global user ID, it can be used effectively to address the challenge of integrating user-related data across system boundaries and to achieve consistent user identification across the enterprise. As a result, single sign-on (SSO) access to multiple SAP services with one authentication and consistent authorization management is possible.

One of the concrete use cases is the SAP Task Center. *2 The SAP Task Center is a service that consolidates workflow approval requests and tasks for users from various SAP applications (for example, SAP SuccessFactors) and displays them in a single inbox. The key here is user consistency and must be ensured that tasks coming from different back-end systems are tied to the same person. A global user ID serves as its common key and relates the tasks gathered from each system to the correct user. Without global user ID harmonization, complex logic is needed to match user IDs across systems, which can lead to missing or misallotted tasks.

 

 

Global user IDs are also important for use scenarios with Joule, our new AI digital assistant. Joule runs on the SAP Business Technology Platform and integrates with multiple back-end systems (such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SuccessFactors) to support users by providing intelligent assistance and insights. *4

 

The assumption is that each system must be able to recognize that a user is the same person. Indeed, in order to activate Joule, there must be a global user ID for the same user on all products that work together. *5 In this way, global user identity is essential as an integrated authentication foundation for cross-sectional services like SAP Task Center and Joule, and is key to smoothly sharing user context across solutions.

 

3. Setup Overview

Here are the high-level steps for implementing Global User Identification and setting up integration with SAP IAS.

  1. Precondition Preparation: First, develop the system environment to take advantage of the Global User ID. Ensure that you have a tenant for SAP Cloud Identity Services and that each SAP product (for example, SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA) is versioned for SAP IAS integration.

  2. Centralize and provision user identity; then design central management of user account information. Synchronize user information using tools such as SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning (commonly referred to as SAP IPS, referred to below as SAP IPS) to consolidate users that exist across different systems. For example, if you want to treat SAP SuccessFactors as the master of users, as an HR system, you can create and grant global user IDs at the same time by provisioning users to the Identity Directory (IdDS) user store via SAP IPS. SCIM provisioning in SAP IPS includes the userUuid attribute (Global User ID) in urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User, which is part of the SAP extension schema, so that all systems can have a common ID. *1
    In this way, provisioning users from the master source to the Identity Directory (IdDS) provides each user with a unified global user ID, and the same identity can be deployed to other systems. In other SAP IPS usage scenarios, patterns such as the synchronization of user email addresses, global user IDs and group affiliations from SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Work Zone (Integrated Portal Service on SAP BTP) may also be taken.

  3. Trust configuration with SAP IAS: Configure trust relationships and user attribute mappings to ensure that SAP IAS is the authentication foundation (IdP) for each system. Specifically, open the SAML 2.0 configuration for each application (connected system) in the SAP IAS Administration Console and configure the attributes to be used for user identification. For authentication linkage with global user IDs, you must ensure that user_uuid (display name “Global User Id”) is included as one of the SAML assertion attributes on the SAP IAS side. If it does not exist by default, you must add the user_uuid attribute as a source with Identity Directory from the Add Custom Attribute feature and map the Global User ID to that value. *6 This setting ensures that when a user is authenticated with SAP IAS, the global user ID is included in the SAML token to uniquely identify the user on the integrated application side. Other attributes, such as Email and Login Name, are included in the assertion as needed, but the global user ID (user_uuid) is the key field for the cross-system user reconciliation process.

  4. Acceptance settings in each system: Finally, you make settings that map users received from SAP IAS correctly on the individual SAP product side. For SAP SuccessFactors, integration authentication with SAP IAS ensures that users in SAP SuccessFactors are associated with global user IDs (IDs generated on SAP IAS are mapped to SAP SuccessFactors users), so no additional configuration is required. Systems such as SAP S/4HANA also set up trust settings with SAP IAS for cloud products, and configure Global User IDs as Name IDs and attributes. In cases like connecting on-premise SAP S/4HANA to SAP Task Center, make sure that the Global User ID (SAPUSER_UUID) is available on the ABAP user management (e.g. SU01) and use it to link the user on the Task Center side. Although the configuration steps vary depending on the specific product, the key point is to ensure that each target system is configured to recognize users based on the Global User ID provided by SAP IAS.

 

4. Issues and considerations for the setup

The challenges and points to note when setting up Global User IDs and SAP IAS are summarized below.

  • Note the confusion of the identity concept: There are various IDs and numbers for each user in the SAP system (for example, user ID, employee ID, person ID, user GUID, and so on). Project members should be aware of the difference between the purpose of the Global User ID and other IDs, and avoid confusion due to similar names.

  • User synchronization and uniqueness: You must avoid the fact that each system issues a separate global user ID for the same user. Global user IDs are automatically generated when users are created in SAP IAS, but if you create the same person separately on different systems, for example, each has a separate global ID. To prevent this, you need to manage your users in central provisioning as described above, or connect them to a single user with a common key such as email address or employee ID when consolidating existing users. In particular, it is safe to first create a user on the SAP IAS side, issue a global user ID, and use it as a flow to link the user to other systems.

  • Wrong attribute mapping: User mapping inconsistencies occur if global user IDs are mapped incorrectly in the connection configuration between SAP IAS and the respective application. For example, if you forget to include user_uuid in the assertion on the SAP IAS side, or if the receiving side has a different attribute (email address, etc.) as the user key, the integration breaks across systems without considering the user as the same person. You should check carefully if the Global User ID is set to use as a user identification axis.

  • User management during production: After the introduction of a global user ID, user lifecycle management must also be kept in mind. When a user enters the company or leaves the company, verify that the user account lifecycle processes in the Identity Directory (IdDS), the user persistency layer of SAP Cloud Identity Services, are properly synchronized with the user management on the part of each business system. For example, if an alumni account is deleted in one system but remains on SAP IAS, there is a risk that unnecessary access remains via a global user ID. In addition, if you are federating your corporate identity provider (e.g. Entra ID / Microsoft Active Directory) and SAP IAS, consider regular synchronization in SAP IPS or automatic provisioning setup on first login, as simply using SAP IAS in proxy mode does not automatically route global user IDs to each system.

 

Conclusion

You now have an overview of global user IDs and SAP IAS configuration points in the SAP environment. When using multiple SAP solutions in an integrated way, a common user foundation with a global user ID is key to success. Please refer to the official SAP Help Portal and the SAP Community guide article to design and configure for your landscape.

 

References

  • *1) SAP Help Portal: User Identity integration scenario with SAP Cloud Identity Services and its configuration guide
     https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/global-user-id-in-integration-scenarios
  • *2) SAP Help Portal: What is the SAP Task Center?
    https://help.sap.com/docs/task-center/sap-task-center/what-is-sap-task-center? locale=en-US
  • *3) SAP Community Blog: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Step-by-Step Guide to Joule Setup Procedures
     https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453
  • *4) SAP Help Portal: What is Joule?
    https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/3fdd7b321eb24d1b9d40605dce822e84/38636457acc346daa4ff7069f041a11a.html
  • *5) SAP Help Portal: Prerequisites for working with SAP products
     https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=services%20tenant%20and%20within%20all,based%20application%20and%20relies%20on
  • *6) SAP Help Portal: Setup instructions for Joule Integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services
    https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=groups%20Groups%20user_uuid%20Global%20User,Save%20your%20configuration

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: SAP Build 新功能：2025年一季度版本亮点
entry.content:
本文本为翻译文，原文档链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights，原作者 Jesse Sukhbayar。

SAP Build 不断发展，利用更多人工智能、灵活性和速度，为现代团队的工作方式提供支持。2025 年第 1 季度，我们针对 SAP Build Code、Process Automation、Apps 和 Work Zone 推出了一系列创新成果，帮助开发人员和业务用户比以往更快地将创意转化为企业级解决方案。

无论您是构建自定义应用、创建 SAP S/4HANA 扩展还是简化流程，这些更新都将帮助您更高效地从概念过渡到价值。  我们来回顾一下从上一季度中选择的前 10 项更新。

 

许可和可用性更新

1.SAP Task Center 现在包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 中

SAP Task Center 现在嵌入在所有 SAP Build Work Zone 版本中，为用户提供来自多个 SAP 系统的工作流任务的统一视图 - 位于其 Work Zone 主页中。无需单独集成。此更新还扩展到合作伙伴许可，使自定义 Work Zone 场景中的 Task Center 更容易。这样，业务用户和管理人员就可以在一个工作区中加快审批速度，集中跟踪任务，并提高工作效率。

  1. SAP Build Apps 现在可在更多区域使用

现在，您可以在 EU11 和悉尼数据中心部署 SAP Build Apps，将应用程序提供给更多用户。增加的覆盖范围可提高应用的响应能力，支持区域特定的合规性需求，并为企业用例提供全局可扩展性。

 

Joule for Developers 

3.使用提示生成端到端工作流

在 SAP Build Process Automation 中，开发人员现在可以使用自然语言描述业务场景，并允许生成性 AI 自动创建表单、决策和流程流。此功能由 SAP 专门构建的大型语言模型 (LLM) 提供支持，可提供基于情境的 SAP 感知结果，从而加快工作流创建速度并减少手动工作量。它还使业务用户能够在无需编写代码的情况下对流程设计做出贡献。

4.从 SAP Build Apps 中的数据生成 UI 页面

SAP Build Apps 现在使用生成式 AI 根据您的数据模型建议用户界面页面。例如，如果您的应用包含 Books、Authors 或 Genres 等实体，Joule 会推荐相应的屏幕、布局和控件。这有助于团队快速从数据建模转变为交互式 UI 设计，缩短原型设计周期并提高设计一致性。

5.在 SAP Build Code 中使用 Generative AI 创建 Fiori UI

开发人员现在可以通过输入命令 /add-ui 或在故事板中选择焦耳图标，在 SAP 构建代码中使用 Joule 为现有 CAP 应用生成基于 Fiori 的 UI。焦耳了解您的应用程序结构，并建议相关的布局、控件和导航模式，帮助您快速使用脚手架前端应用，并专注于定制，而不是从头开始。

6.CAP 应用生成的多代理支持

SAP Build Code 现在使用由 Joule 支持的多代理支持来生成完整的 CAP 应用程序。每个代理都专注于特定部分（如数据模型、服务或 UI），因此您可以从单个提示中获取更完整的项目。此增强节省了时间，可帮助您关注业务逻辑，而不是从头开始构建所有内容。

另请阅读 SAP Build Code 中的所有 GenAI 选项概览 ， david_bizer .

 

增强 SAP Business Suite 应用程序

7.使用自定义 UI 扩展 SAP S/4HANA（计划即将发布）

即将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）中推出，开发人员将能够使用 SAP Build 可扩展性向导创建并排扩展 - 通过定制现有 SAP Fiori 应用或构建全新的 Web 应用程序。这种方法使团队能够满足特定的业务需求，同时保留核心系统，支持精简核心原则和长期灵活性。

  • 请参阅文档：使用可扩展性向导。
  • 在设置中，请阅读博客 @Karthik-Thiru 提供的 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公共版。 

8.SAP Build 引导式体验的新增强功能

SAP Build 对引导式体验进行了新的增强，有助于简化复杂的流程，如员工入职、育儿假、采购审批和 IT 服务管理，其中涉及多个步骤、系统和利益相关方。 在此版本中，现在可以在 SAP Build Store 中获取 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding 和转岗的即用型内容包，从而更快地为新员工和内部调动提供一致的逐步指导。

  • 开始： SAP Build 引导式体验 ，florian_buech
  • 观看视频（2 分钟）：借助 SAP Build 引导式体验，规划育儿假。

9.实时工作流的中间消息事件

通过对中间消息事件的新支持，SAP Build Process Automation 允许工作流暂停，直到自动接收并恢复特定 API触发的输入为止。这种强大的功能可减少等待时间和手动监控，帮助团队设计更具适应性和响应能力的流程，同时减少错误和开销。 

 

提高开发人员工作效率

10.SAP Build Code 中改进的项目概览

SAP Build Code 中重新设计的项目概览提供了所有应用组件的基于树的清洁视图，支持 CAP Java 和 CAP 节点。此增强的布局与故事板集成，使开发人员能够更轻松地导航复杂项目、启用新团队成员并在构建时保持专注度。

了解更多

有关新功能的完整列表，请查看以下资源：

  • SAP Build 帮助门户
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

entry.title: Introducing the New Project Creation Wizard within SAP Build Lobby
entry.content:
Introduction:

We are pleased to announce the launch of an enhanced Project Creation Wizard in the SAP Build Lobby, designed to accelerate and streamline your project initiation processes. This new tool reflects our commitment to incorporating user feedback and optimizing your interaction with SAP Build. 

Background: 

To better understand user needs, we conducted extensive research involving interviews with a diverse group of users.  

A common theme in user feedback highlighted the need for a more intuitive project creation flow, starting with the user's objective before progressing to tool selection. Users expressed interest in new capabilities directly accessible from the Lobby, such as Node.js Full Stack and Java Full Stack options. 

Different Project Creation Options

Our findings guided us in redesigning the project creation process to align with user preferences and streamline the initial steps. 

What’s New? 

  • Intent-Based Navigation: The redesigned Project Creation experience prioritizes the user's goals and required tasks. Relevant project types are grouped logically to facilitate easier and faster selection, ensuring intuitive project management. 
  • Consistent Experience: In line with Fiori UX guidelines, we offer a seamless and consistent experience across the Project Creation Wizard and Extensibility Wizard. This step-by-step approach provides clarity and guidance, helping users make informed choices regarding their unique scenarios. 
  • Modern Look & Feel: Project creation options are categorized by Objectives, Categories, and Types, assisting developers in locating the right tools promptly. This organization allows for future expansion with additional capabilities. 

Next Steps: 

We value your input and invite you to share your experience with the new Project Creation Wizard. Your feedback is crucial for continued improvement. Use the "Share Product Feedback" option and click the "Give Feedback" button to let us know how this updated experience supports your professional objectives. 

Feedback

 

For more information, please visit SAP Help Documentation. 

We look forward to enhancing your project creation journey with SAP Build and ensuring our tools meet your business needs effectively. 

Already tried it? Please feel free to share your thoughts also in the comments below!

entry.title: Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers
entry.content:
As SAP is evolving its Business Suite, AI, and Data Products to meet rising market demands, and as part of this strategy, we are introducing a new exciting, unified commercial model for SAP Build. This updated offering consolidates capabilities for pro-code, low code and AI powered development tools making it easier than ever to build applications and extensions within the SAP ecosystem.

With built-in access to runtime, automation, and extensibility services, SAP Build now empowers both business users and professional developers to choose the right tool set for the right job. The integration of generative AI enhances productivity by streamlining workflows, automating tasks, and accelerating decision-making.

To further support development, SAP Build capabilities along with SAP HANA Cloud are now fully included in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Packages empowering customers to handle the majority of their expected scenarios right out of the box. Read the full announcement here.

What changes?

We've heard our customers loud and clear; they asked for simpler licensing, easier provisioning, and a unified toolset. Previously, each SAP Build product like Build Apps, Process Automation, Work Zone, Build Code, and ABAP was licensed separately. This resulted in having lots of different services for customers to manage.  

The following list of 14 services from SAP Build have now been consolidated:

SAP Build Services:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Mobile Services
  • Application Frontend Service
  • SAP Business Application Studio

 SAP Build Runtime Services:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • SAP Document Management Service
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management
  • SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

 Now you can access the full range of SAP Build capabilities listed above through just four distinct licenses:

SAP Build base user

This edition is for users who need to access applications, gain insights for day-to-day business needs, and approve tasks. SAP Build base user includes:

  • Access to digital workspaces
  • Access web and mobile apps
  • Request and approve workflow tasks
  • Access mobile start
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

 SAP Build premium user

This edition is for users who author content, monitor processes, customize pages, and deploy bots. SAP Build premium user includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build base user, plus
  • Monitor processes via the dashboard
  • Deploy attended robotic process automation bots
  • Author and access workspace content
  • Visually design digital workspaces and guided experiences
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

SAP Build developer

This edition is for users who develop application/extensions, workflows, and automations for S/4 and other LoB apps. SAP Build developer includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build premium user, plus
  • Build with low-code/no-code developer tools
  • Develop with code-first developer tools (IDEs)
  • Create and deploy apps and extensions
  • Develop and deploy mobile applications
  • Design and deploy workflows
  • 5000 API Calls
  • 500 MB Storage

SAP Build runtime

Required for application-hosting and lifecycle management services that support the creation and execution for dev/test as well as production environments. SAP Build runtime includes:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • Application runtime
  • Document management storage and API
  • Cloud transport management
  • Continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)

Note: Customers can access applications hosted on runtimes without using any end-user capabilities. This ensures customers aren't charged for user licenses they don’t actively use.

Find detailed information about SAP Build and what’s in scope on the SAP Help Portal. 

What are the key benefits of the new SAP Build packaging, and how will it help our customers?

The new SAP Build packaging delivers a streamlined, powerful experience for building extensions, automations, and apps across the SAP ecosystem. Here's how it benefits customers:

  • Unified Access Across Tools: SAP Build now brings together low-code, pro-code, and AI-powered development tools under a single license removing the complexity of managing multiple products and licenses.
  • Built-In ERP Integration: SAP Build and SAP HANA Cloud entitlements are now included in SAP Cloud ERP Private Packages, enabling faster innovation and development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.
  • Empowerment for Developers & Business Users: Both professional developers and key users gain the flexibility to personalize SAP systems with greater speed up to 3x faster, according to
  • Optimized Cost & Efficiency: A flexible, usage-based runtime model helps reduce total cost of ownership and streamline operational overhead.
  • Simplified Governance: A unified licensing approach improves compliance management and centralizes governance across all tools.
  • Business Value First: By removing commercial and technical barriers, organizations can focus more on driving transformation and delivering real business outcomes.

With SAP Build, customers can:

  • Scale confidently within the SAP ecosystem: Use included licenses to rapidly build compliant extensions that align with SAP standards and scale across the enterprise.
  • Leverage AI to boost productivity: Automate complex cross-system processes with AI agents, customize Joule with skill-specific tasks, and speed up development using SAP-optimized AI tools.
  • Deliver faster from front-end to full-stack: Build complete extensions with prebuilt templates, low-code tools, and integrated services like authentication, deployment, and lifecycle management—all on a single, seamless platform.

 Commercial Model and Availability

As of 22nd April 2025, SAP Build is available to customers for subscription only. Availability for consumption under BTPEA and PayGo is anticipated by the end of Q2.

Call to action

  • Visit the SAP Build pricing page to learn more
  • Help portal documentation
  • Register for the SAP Build webinar on May 8th
  • Read the full announcement here
  • Visit the SAP Discovery Center for more details.
  • Review pricing on Discovery Center

entry.title: Partners unlock innovation with latest SAP Build capabilities
entry.content:
20 Partners from 10 countries participated and built innovative solutions during the recently concluded Hack2Build on SAP Build. With the latest SAP Build capabilities delivered recently by SAP, Partners explored and leveraged key functionalities of the low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools for building their innovations.

Partners extend, create, and automate with SAP Build

In this Hack2Build edition, partner used SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Process Automation integration and built applications to guide users through a multi-step process and efficient completion of complex business processes. Partners also used SAP Build Code, the AI-powered code assistance for application creation and code optimization.

The hack phase started on April 8,2025, and concluded on April 15, 2025, with two demo jams. The demo jam saw the partners present their innovations developed using SAP Build in just five working days. A panel of judges chose three winners each, impressed by high-powered pitches.

Hack2Build SAP Build-Partners who participated in the final demo jamIn this blog, we will highlight the three winners from each of the two groups who showcased their innovations developed on SAP Build.

Group 1 Winners

Hack2Build SAP Build - Group1 winners

 Jupiter 2000 showcased a Building Facade Inspection Safety Program (FISP) Application enhancing building inspections with AI-generated suggestions and with guided experience report submission. The guided experience offers inspectors to fill out reports accurately and in compliance with regulations. Using SAP AI Core, AI-Powered photo identification for crack detection and image recognition to generate automatically description of facade defects with manual override as needed. The application allows for reviewing, editing, and submitting inspection reports before final approval with a possibility of submission to government departments for streamlined reporting.

Jupiter 2000's Façade Inspection Safety Program (FISP) Application

Reply introduced NextGen Service Portal designed to improve service request reporting by providing a guided, web-based experience for reporting service tickets using SAP Work Zone and SAP Build Apps. It is a pre-configured guided solution for service-intensive industries which allows internal employees, external partners, and even unknown users to report issues through a simple visual and structured web-based experience. With a guided step-by-step process to report service tickets, structured data input, capability of interactive floor maps and upload of photos, the data can flow directly to SAP Service Cloud for real-time ticket management.

NextGen Service Portal by Reply

EY developed Smart Automation of Goods Receipts and Purchase Order creation with a central SAP Work Zone site and SAP Build Apps for purchase order (PO) creation, and goods receipt QR Code scanning which eliminates the need for third-party scanner tools, utilizing AI for planning suggestions. The solution eliminates the need for third-party scanner tools, offers a Clean Core compliant and AI-integrated process, with automated and Instant creation of Goods Receipts (GR) or Goods Issues (GI) in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing efficiency and flexibility for warehouse staff using mobile devices.

EY's Smart Automation of Goods Receipts and Purchase Order creation

Group 2 Winners

Hack2Build SAP Build - Group2 winners

MOURI Tech developed Guide2Resolve, a complaint resolution solution to streamline complaints at large events like expos and provide enhanced attendee experience and improved organizational efficiency. Guide2Resolve simplifies and accelerates the complaint submission and resolution process using a guided Process, structured, and automated flow powered by SAP Build Work Zone and UI Integration Cards. Generative AI determines category based on issue description.

Guide2Resolve application by MOURI Tech

Cognizant showcased their Case management AI Agent for SAP. They offer a side by side extensibility solution that adheres to SAP clean core principles designed to automate processes, provides user guidance, and improves efficiency, particularly notable in the utilities sector. The solution brings a unified view of all exceptions, and offers case resolution using guided procedures, utilizes SAP Build, Work Zone, and Process Automation to create an efficient digital workspace. The solution harnesses the power of generative AI to deliver a suite of multi-agents that transform case management.

Case Management AI Agent for SAP by Cognizant

NTT Data’s agri business solution seeks to improve the integration of large-scale weighing operations, crucial for grain transportation. The application receive weights and quality analysis upon soft commodity movements, indicate which trading contract should be used, allows automatic or manual application to contracts, executes goods movement and issues invoicing documents. The solution developed with SAP Build Work Zone with the support of Joule during the development process, utilizes SAP CAP with SAP HANA HDI Containers with a custom SAPUI5 front end. The solution connects to SAP S/4HANA applications by the SAP Cloud Connector.

 NTT Agro – Scale and application

Judges 

Many thanks to the panel of judges who spent considerable time to evaluate and share feedback on the innovative solutions presented by the partners.

Pavel PenazGlobal Vice President & Head of Partner Solution Success BTP & AI, SAP Czech Republic
Kathrin HettelVice President, Strategic Cross-Portfolio & AI Partnership, SAP SE
Julia IvanovaSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Marc HuberSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Sruthi ChiramelSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Stephan KoenigSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Neeraj UpretiSAP Ecosystem Development Manager, SAP America

Special Thanks

We extend our appreciation to the SAP team members from SAP Build Product Management and Partner Solution Success, who coached the partners to successfully develop their solutions during the Hack2Build SAP Build initiative.

Next Phase

Partners have expressed their interest to complete their full scope solution in 10 weeks. Partner Solution Success will engage with all the participating partners to support their build journey in the next weeks and get their innovations ready for customer adoption.

entry.title: Kickstart Your Developer Journey – Get SAP-Certified in Low-Code/No-Code
entry.content:
New to development? No problem! SAP offers a certification and learning resources that help you build real business apps, workflows, and user experiences — without needing to be a coding expert. Using tools like SAP Build Apps, Work Zone, and Process Automation, you’ll be surprised how much you can do just by dragging, dropping, and configuring.

 

Simple Steps to Get Certified

 

Step 1: Learn at Your Own Pace Follow these beginner-friendly learning paths:

  • SAP Build Apps: Build apps visually without needing to write code. Learn how to connect data, create user-friendly pages, and define app behavior.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Set up a digital workspace where users can access their apps and content in one place.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to build simple workflows and automation tools to save time and reduce manual work.

Step 2: Practice What You Learn You’ll try a real-world simulation using everything you’ve learned. This hands-on practice is required before taking the certification exam and helps boost your confidence.

Step 3: Take the Certification Exam Once you feel ready, book your exam. Passing it proves your skills and gives you an official SAP Certification — a great addition to your resume!

 

Want to Keep Growing?

 

  • Watch tutorials on YouTube from SAP Developer Advocates.
  • Join local SAP CodeJam events to meet and learn from other developers.
  • Subscribe to SAP Learning Hub for more guided learning from SAP experts.

 

Whether you're starting your career or switching to tech, this certification is a great way to get hands-on with real business tools — and build your future.

entry.title: Build, Configure, and Deploy SAP CAP Projects to the Cloud
entry.content:
🚀A Comprehensive, Step-by-Step Guide to Creating and Deploying a Full CAP Application with SAP Fiori UI in SAP Business Application Studio

The Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model provided by SAP is a powerful, opinionated framework designed to streamline the process of building enterprise-grade services and applications that are scalable, extensible, and cloud-ready. In this extensive guide, we will delve deeply into each and every step required to build a full-fledged CAP project from scratch using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), enhance it with a Fiori UI, and deploy the entire application stack to the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) on Cloud Foundry. This guide is structured with elaborate explanations for beginners and intermediate developers alike.

Prerequisites and Initial Setup

Before we begin, ensure you have a valid SAP BTP trial or productive account with appropriate entitlements, particularly for SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Build Work Zone, which are essential for running managed application routers and deploying HTML5 applications. You also need access to SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), which will serve as your cloud-based IDE for this project.

== 1. Creating a Dev Space in SAP Business Application Studio ==

  1. Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Business Application Studio.

  2. On the landing page, click on the "Create Dev Space" button, which will prompt you to select a template.

  3. From the available options, choose "Full-Stack Cloud Application" as it comes pre-configured with essential tools for CAP and Fiori development.

  4. Assign a recognizable name to your dev space, for example, cap-bookshop, to easily identify it later.

  5. Check the box labeled "SAP HANA Tools" to enable support for creating and managing HANA database artifacts.

  6. Click on "Create Dev Space" and wait patiently for the status of the space to change to "RUNNING."

  7. Once the dev space is up and running, click on it to launch the integrated development environment where you will build your application.

== 2. Provisioning a SAP HANA Cloud Instance ==

  1. Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit using your credentials.

  2. Navigate into your global account and select the appropriate subaccount.

  3. Under the navigation pane, click on "Service Marketplace" to view the available services.

  4. Search for "SAP HANA Cloud" and click on it when it appears in the search results.

  5. Click on the "Create" button to begin provisioning a new HANA database instance.

  6. Choose the SAP HANA Database (ensure you do not select SAP HANA Cloud Tools).

  7. Sign-In with cloud foundry account and select your space from the dropdown
  8. Enter a unique name for the instance (e.g., hana), specify your required configurations, set an admin password.

  9. Administrator Section:

    • Administrator Username: Auto-filled as DBADMIN.

    • Administrator Password: Enter a secure password.

    • Confirm Password: Retype to confirm.
      🔐Keep in mind: Passwords for DBADMIN expire every 180 days, so set a reminder to change them before expiry.

  11. Configure SAP HANA Database Settings

    In this step, you'll define your database instance's technical specifications.

     

    • Available Memory Range:
      In the trial version, it's fixed at 16 GB.

    ℹ️Note: Trial accounts come with fixed configurations for memory and storage. For more flexibility, a commercial account is required.

  12. Network & Cloud Connector Configuration

    In this step, configure the network access and optionally enable the Cloud Connector if needed.

    Allow all IP addresses (Selected in this guide)
    Suitable for trial and development — easiest for testing external tools.
  13. Click "Create" in final stage

  14. The provisioning process may take a few minutes to complete. Once done, your database will be ready for binding.

  15. (Important) Whenever trying to deploy or bind your service or application ensure HANA Cloud is Started and running

== 3. Creating a New CAP Project Using Terminal Commands ==

  1. Inside your running BAS dev space, open a new terminal window by navigating to the menu: Terminal → New Terminal.

  2. Change the current directory to your desired workspace, typically located at /home/user/projects/:

cd /home/user/projects/

  1. Run the CAP project initialization command:

cds init bookshop
cd bookshop
